content:
- Pluxee Check-in with Ramki - Monthly Office Hours (Digital Twin Structured Summary)

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- This Pluxee monthly governance check-in with Ramki was a detailed operational review ahead of an upcoming client meeting. The discussion covered progress on outstanding action items from previous governance cycles and current sprint delivery across product, run, and customer service tracks. The session involved updates on code, feature delivery, incident handling, system monitoring, and specific delivery challenges with various modules (mobile app stack, performance center, SRE transitions, etc.). Ramki offered direct guidance on process and methodology—emphasizing streamlined action tracking, reporting automation, and enforcing standardization across internal presentations and dashboards. Integration and support for ongoing product and client requirements, their impacts on teams, and technical approaches (API design, adapter layers, SDK/iframe choices, etc.) were also reviewed, with clear instructions from Ramki for next steps, especially on collaboration boundaries and role clarity.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- Action Items Framed as Project Plans: Ramki emphasized that tracked items should roll up into a coherent project plan, not as fragmented tasks (“action item should only be translated to… project plan”).
- Monitoring & Incident Handling Enhanced: Production monitoring was intensified over the holiday period, and exceptional metrics are to be regularized into ongoing dashboards (“regular monitoring should incorporate whatever you have been monitoring in this P covers”).
- Staffing/Resourcing Adjustments Ongoing: Team configuration adjustments (especially on build/sustenance and SRE) are in progress, with focus on ensuring no revenue impact and responsibilities are balanced.
- Standardization & Automation Mandated: Ramki mandated that governance decks and presentation content for internal review be script-automated, drawing from source-of-truth (Jira/zero data), and format standardization established (“presentations decks auto-generated… not manual error”).
- Clear Stakeholder Guidance on API/Integration: Future integrations (Electron/Tachyon/mobile app clients) should be solely exposed via a defined adapter/API layer (e.g., Orient/GraphQL), avoiding direct product-layer entanglement; clarity on stance regarding SDKs vs. iframe support.
- Disciplined Ticketing and Diligence Expected: Ramki called for rigorous separation of bug/idea tickets, noting diligence is not a bandwidth issue; accurate categorization is required.
- Metric Visualizations & Reporting Critiqued: Guidance on graphing conventions (time series X-axis, clarity of SLO trends, rationale for observed data dips), ensuring alignment with common dashboarding logic.

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. On Structuring and Tracking Project Progress:
- Ramki was insistent that action items and deliverables must be rolled up as project plans with clear structure, demarcating subtasks as part of the overarching initiative. He reiterated the need for consolidated tracking, underscoring that process steps should not multiply superficial tracking items but ensure clarity of ownership (“this is the project plan…these 40 things can be tracked as subproject…just do this as a summary action item”).

2. On Monitoring, Automation, and Reporting:
- Ramki demonstrated a strong preference for automating and regularizing all exceptional/holiday-specific monitoring into standard production dashboards, questioning why key metrics like TPS are not standard. He pushed for script-driven, automated presentation decks for operational summaries, noting that reliance on manual snapshots introduces errors and inhibits predictability and transparency.

3. On Internal Deck Standardization and Consumption:
- Ramki demanded that internal governance formats be standardized for regular stakeholder presentation, and that all decks be generated from canonical data sources (explicitly differentiating deck formats for internal governance vs. client usage). He articulated the risk of data drift, context loss, and inefficiencies driven by disparate formats.

4. On Division of Responsibilities and API Strategy:
- For integration requirements (e.g., Electron/Tachyon for Pluxee mobile), Ramki clarified an architectural boundary: all necessary logic should be exposed via a single adapter/API layer (Orient) rather than requiring deep SME engagement per product. He endorsed future-looking approaches like using GraphQL and discouraged repeating product- or app-specific logic for each client. He asserted parallelism with similar integrations (e.g., Optum) and required synchronization/across approaches.

5. On Ticketing Rigor and Team Diligence:
- Ramki pressed the team on precise ticket categorization, challenging the notion that process diligence is a bandwidth constraint. He stressed “no time difference between doing a job and doing the job in the right place…diligence matter.”

6. On UI, Security, and Customer-Facing Features:
- He questioned the value of duplicating pop-up/business requirements for web where user bases are marginal, urging data-driven de-scoping as appropriate. On SDK/API approaches, he expressed an explicit stance against supporting mobile SDKs, favoring iframe embeds for portability and sustainability—articulating the reasoning forcefully (“we have long gone out of it…iframe is the best we can do”).

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

1. Task: Consolidate all action items as part of coherent project plans/subprojects; avoid scattered checklists.
- Assigned To: All relevant module/project/track leads
- Instructions: Structure project tracking as plans with clear breakdowns, tracking sub-items as subprojects. Ensure high-level summary for each action stream.
- Timeline: Immediate/ongoing

2. Task: Regularize exceptional (holiday) monitoring metrics into standard dashboards (e.g., TPS, infra metrics).
- Assigned To: Chandan Maheshwari (and performance/monitoring teams)
- Instructions: Integrate all critical/exceptional monitoring previously instituted into everyday tracking dashboards; ensure no drop in visibility post-holiday period.
- Timeline: Next review cycle

3. Task: Automate and standardize reporting decks, especially operational predictability/quality reports, entirely via scripts from primary data sources (e.g., Jira, zero data).
- Assigned To: Veda, Balaji, relevant reporting/ops leads
- Instructions: All summary decks must be script-generated; eliminate manual snapshots; internal governance deck formats to be standardized and adopted universally.
- Timeline: By end of January

4. Task: Expose only necessary APIs for client integrations under a defined adapter/API (Orient), and consider GraphQL approach moving forward; do not involve Tachyon SMEs unless strictly necessary.
- Assigned To: Electron/Integration/API architects (Veda, Balaji, Electron team)
- Instructions: Collate required mobile APIs under Orient; offer GraphQL or similar adapter; avoid deep Tachyon team involvement; leverage Optum integration learnings for harmonization.
- Timeline: Before upcoming client workshop and major mobile integration planning

5. Task: Ensure proper categorization and diligence in bug vs. idea ticket management; educate teams as needed, but hold the line on precise ticket filing.
- Assigned To: All product/engineering leads; reinforced by Balaji
- Instructions: Do not tolerate mislabeling of bugs as ideas; file tickets accurately and timely; reinforce process rigor as a function of discipline, not bandwidth.
- Timeline: Immediate

6. Task: For security/customer-experience changes (e.g., card controls/pop-ups), revisit requirements for web vs. mobile on user base justification; optimize efforts on actual usage impact.
- Assigned To: Product owners (web and mobile)
- Instructions: Deprioritize low-impact features for marginal user groups; validate requirements with marketing/business owners before committing engineering resources.
- Timeline: Prior to next sprint/feature planning

- VII. Jira Ticket

Scope, Assignee, Justification:

1. Ticket: Script Automation for Operational Reporting Decks
- Scope: Automate generation of predictability and quality summary decks (for governance forums) using Jira/zero data, ensuring format standardization, error-free representation, and canonical data flow.
- Assignee: Veda (with Balaji’s oversight), reporting/DevOps support as relevant
- Justification: Mandated by Ramki to eliminate manual errors, increase predictability/visibility, and align all internal forums on a single truth; duplicate manual and non-canonical decks to be phased out. High-impact, persistent benefit for all governance cycles.

2. Ticket: Integration of Exceptional Monitoring Metrics into Standard Dashboards
- Scope: Expand standard dashboards to incorporate holiday/exceptional monitoring metrics (TPS data, infra capacity, etc.) as ongoing components, ensuring full production coverage.
- Assignee: Chandan Maheshwari (and respective monitoring/infra team)
- Justification: Addresses Ramki’s directive to avoid “ball drops” in routine monitoring; ensures readiness/visibility for future scale events without ad-hoc preparations. Elevated operational resilience.

3. Ticket: Define & Implement Orient Layer API (w/ GraphQL Adapter) for Client Mobile Integrations
- Scope: Identify all APIs necessary for Pluxee (and similar client) mobile app integrations, expose through Orient (with possible GraphQL adapter), and document as reference for future projects (e.g., Optum). Decouple direct Tachyon SME involvement.
- Assignee: Electron Integration/API architects (Veda/electron middleware owners)
- Justification: Ramki’s boundary-setting on architectural separation; enables scalable integration, reuse, and lowers SME overhead across parallel client engagements.

- No tickets for general action stream realignments or ticketing diligence as these are process enforcements, not bounded deliverables.

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

- Assess and communicate progress on automating operational governance decks before next review; ensure any blockers are surfaced early.
- Confirm with monitoring teams that all newly integrated holiday/exceptional metrics display accurately in dashboards and alerting flows.
- Align API catalog (for Orient/GraphQL layer) with past Optum and current Pluxee requirements; schedule technical walkthroughs to validate any functional gaps.
- Verify and report on the impact of proposed reconfigurations (staffing, role split, SRE transitions) on project velocity and accountability, to anticipate/discuss any upcoming delivery risks.
- Rationalize requirements for web/mobile feature parity—present clear data on usage and stakeholder impact before developing features with marginal demand.
- Monitor for any drift between ticket categorization standards and real practice; escalate promptly if process gaps persist.

entities: Pluxee, project plan, action items, mobile decommissioning, Apollo Connect, Mario, UI overhaul, SLO/SLA tracking, dashboards, incident management, staffing, team reconfiguration, SRE transition, Accenture transition, OND/JFM planning, predictability/quality reports, bug/idea ticketing, KYC categorization, card/transaction policy, API/adapter layers, Electron, Tachyon, Orient, GraphQL, client workshops, Bharat QR, closed loop, open loop, incident (SEV), reporting standardization, automations

title: 2025-01-09_Pluxee Check-in with Ramki - Monthly (2)
